headline: The Bombers Just Pulled a Thanos, and We Need to Talk About It
subheadline: Week 12 brought us a 9-1 beatdown so brutal it should require a content warning, plus the most predictable upset that somehow still surprised everyone.
body:
Look, I've been doing this long enough to know when a team is just *different*. The **Albuquerque Bombers** dropping a 9.0-1.0 nuke on the **Benchwarmers** isn't just winning—it's the fantasy baseball equivalent of the 2017 Warriors playing a YMCA rec league. (Side note: calling yourself the Benchwarmers and then getting absolutely torched feels like nominative determinism at its finest. Rob Schneider would be proud. Or confused. Probably both.)

This was the kind of massacre that makes you text your group chat at 11 PM like "are we even watching the same sport?" The Bombers went full Heisenberg—they are the ones who knock, and this week they kicked down the entire door.

Meanwhile, **BaseballPro77** handled **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** 6.0-3.0, and I have thoughts. First: the Wonderdogs (great Bob's Burgers reference, genuinely respect it) came into this week looking playoff-caliber. Second: BaseballPro77 is the kind of team name that screams "I created this account in 2003 and never looked back." And somehow that guy just quietly dismantled a contender? It's giving me major 2011 Mavericks energy—nobody sees them coming because nobody's watching.

**HVR Hits Different** continues to, well, hit different with an 8.0-2.0 demolition of **happymomof4@Y!.com**. (And yes, every time I type that team name, I think about the person who definitely set this up at their kid's baseball game while also checking a Yahoo email from 2008. Respect the hustle, but maybe update your brand?)

The **Houston Trashcan Decoders** edged **Will Smith's Right Hand** 6.0-4.0, which is objectively the funniest sentence I've written all year. Both team names are *chef's kiss*—one referencing baseball's greatest scandal, the other the Oscars' most chaotic moment. We're living in the golden age of petty naming.

Now here's where things get weird: the standings show everyone at 0-0 with zero points for. Either our stats guy had a rough week (we've all been there), or Week 12 exists in some kind of fantasy purgatory where nothing counts and the points don't matter. It's very *Whose Line Is It Anyway* energy, which honestly might be the most accurate description of this entire league.

With playoffs presumably looming (assuming our standings ever update), the Bombers look unstoppable. Everyone else? Just trying not to become their next victim.

See you in Week 13. Bring snacks. Maybe a helmet.
